title: [nightly build] Incorrect handling with CTE and EXIST
description:
### What happens?

Hi, I found that DuckDB will have a different handling of CTE with EXIST clause from [SQLite](https://www.db-fiddle.com/f/nuX6oJBVMXkb1MYgWVfGX6/0) and [Postgres](https://www.db-fiddle.com/f/s7BU9XTJzREC1motGXtAJi/0).

In this example, both SQLite and Postgres return 1 row, while DuckDB returns zero rows.

```sql
CREATE  TABLE  t1(c0 BOOLEAN);
CREATE  TABLE  t2(c0 BOOLEAN);
CREATE  TABLE  t3(c0 BOOLEAN);
INSERT INTO t2(c0) VALUES (true);
INSERT INTO t3(c0) VALUES (true);

WITH c AS (
  SELECT *
  FROM t1
  WHERE false
)
SELECT t2.c0
FROM t2 CROSS JOIN t3 CROSS JOIN c
UNION ALL
SELECT t2.c0 FROM t2 CROSS JOIN t3
WHERE NOT EXISTS (
  SELECT 1
  FROM c
); -- {0 rows}
```

After some initial analysis, I found the following query can return the correct result. Note that the only difference here is that this query uses the physical empty table instead of an empty CTE. And both SQLite and Postgres also return 1 row for this case, which means there might be a bug with the CTE EXIST clause. 

```sql
SELECT t2.c0
FROM t2 CROSS JOIN t3 CROSS JOIN t1
UNION ALL
SELECT t2.c0 FROM t2 CROSS JOIN t3
WHERE NOT EXISTS (
  SELECT 1
  FROM t1
); -- {1 row}
```

### To Reproduce

Using `DuckDB v1.4.0-dev2537 (Development Version) 448f32f03e` cli, run the above queries.

### OS:

Ubuntu 24.04.1 LTS, x86_64, Linux 6.14.0-1010-aws (AWS EC2)

### DuckDB Version:

v1.4.0-dev2537 (Development Version) 448f32f03e

### DuckDB Client:

CLI

### Hardware:

AWS EC2 instance

### Full Name:

Qiuyang Mang

### Affiliation:

UC Berkeley

### What is the latest build you tested with? If possible, we recommend testing with the latest nightly build.

I have tested with a nightly build

### Did you include all relevant data sets for reproducing the issue?

Yes

### Did you include all code required to reproduce the issue?

- [x] Yes, I have

### Did you include all relevant configuration (e.g., CPU architecture, Python version, Linux distribution) to reproduce the issue?

- [x] Yes, I have
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t1(c0 BOOLEAN, c1 INT);
CREATE TABLE t2(c0 BOOLEAN, c1 VARCHAR(10));
CREATE TABLE t3(c0 BOOLEAN, c1 FLOAT);
SELECT t2.c0 FROM t2 CROSS JOIN t3 CROSS JOIN t1 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM t1 WHERE c0 IS NULL);
key_bug_features: Incorrect handling of CTE with EXIST clause
